title: Removing the spinal column from a half-carcass of a slaughtered animal
abstract: The present invention relates to the slaughtering industry where slaughtered animals are cut along the median plane to provide a pair of half-carcasses (1A), and the spinal column (3), also referred to as the vertebral column, is removed from the half-carcasses.
claims: Claims 1. A method of removing, at least partly, the spinal column from a half- carcass (1A, IB) of a slaughtered animal that has been cut substantially along the median plane (2) of the slaughtered animal thereby providing a median cut surface (6) of the half-carcass (1A, IB), the method comprising - locating, on the ventral side of the half-carcass (1A, IB), an anatomical structure (7, 17) defined in relation to joints between rib heads and the spinal column (3) of the half-carcass (1A, IB) and/or in relation to the concavity between the spinal column and the transverse process (18), - determining a characteristic geometrical feature (15) of the median cut surface (6) and/or of the ventral side of the half-carcass (1A, IB), the characteristic geometrical feature (15) comprising : o a position on and/or a spatial orientation of at least a part of the exposed rib (4) or spinal column (3) or transverse process (18), and - cutting along a cutting path (13), the cutting path being defined in relation to said anatomical structure (7, 17) and said characteristic geometrical feature (15) of the median cut surface (6) and/or of the ventral side of the half-carcass, so as to remove, at least partly, the spinal column from the half-carcass (1A, IB). 2. The method according to claim 1, wherein the cutting path (13) passes through a position being offset a predetermined first offset to the located anatomical feature (7, 17) and a position in the half-carcass (1A, IB) being offset a predetermined second offset from the characteristic geometrical feature (15). 3. The method according to claim 2, wherein the characteristic geometrical feature is a spatial orientation, such as/or the spatial orientation of the median cut surface (6), and wherein the second offset is a predetermined angle (V) with the characteristic geometrical feature. 4. The method according to claim 2, wherein the characteristic geometrical feature is a position and wherein the second offset is a predetermined orientated distance from the characteristic geometrical feature (15). 5. The method according to any of the claims 2-4, wherein the first and/or the second offset is equal to zero. 6 The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the anatomical structure (7, 17) is a concavity defined in relation to the joint between rib heads and thoracic vertebrae, top of rib heads, or a concavity below the rib head (17). 7. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the characteristic geometrical feature (15) is: spinous process, the spatial orientation of the median cut surface (6), - spinal canal (5), the upper most position of the spinal column (16), or the tangent to the surface between the upper most position of the spinal column (16) and the anatomical structure (7, 17). 8. The method according to claim 1, wherein the anatomical structure located is a concavity (7, 17) defined in relation to joints between rib heads and the spinal column (3) of the half-carcass (1A, IB), - the characteristic geometrical feature (15) determined is the spatial orientation of the median cut surface (6), and - the cutting path (13) intersects the concavity (7, 17) or intersects a predetermined first offset therefrom and forming a predetermined angle (V) with the median cut surface (6) so as to remove, at least partly, the spinal column from the half-carcass (1A, IB). 9. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein locating the anatomical structure is done using optical and/or mechanical locator/means providing data on a zone of the half-carcass, preferably including at least the concavity (7, 17). 10. The method according to any of the preceding claims wherein characteristic geometrical feature (15) is determined using optical and/or mechanical locator/means. 11. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the anatomical feature is located using optical and/or mechanical locator/means. 12. The method according to any of the preceding claims, comprising moving the half-carcass, while supported by a conveyer, along a longitudinal direction of the spinal column (3), illuminating the ventral side of the moving half-carcass, and obtaining image data on the zone of the half-carcass, preferably including at least the concavity (7). 13. A method according to claim 12, wherein illuminating the ventral side of the half-carcass (1A, IB) includes projecting a beam of light defining a plane of light transverse to the direction of movement, and receiving projected light reflected from the half-carcass (1A, IB) with a camera situated outside the plane of light to obtain profile information on the zone of the half-carcass, preferably including at least the concavity. 14. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the half- carcass is arranged on a conveyor and wherein the position of the half-carcass on the conveyor is stabilized. 15. The method according to any one of claims 12-15 wherein the half-carcass is arranged suspended in an orientation, the method further comprising illuminating the ventral side of the half-carcass (1A, IB) by projecting a beam of light defining a plane of light transverse to the longitudinal direction of the spinal column, and receiving projected light reflected from the half-carcass (1A, IB) with a camera situated outside the plane of light to obtain profile information on the zone of the half-carcass including at least the concavity. 16. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the half- carcass is arranged on a conveyer, said conveyer comprising an encoder providing data on movements of the conveyer and thereby the half-carcass.
